options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: starting_area
options.description:
Which chunks are available at the start. The player may need to move through locked chunks to reach the starting
               area, but any areas that require quests, skills, or coins are not available as a starting location

               NOTE: MEMBERS LOGIC ISSUE: WE DON'T ACTUALLY CARE ABOUT WHAT YOUR START COULD BE HAVE FUN!

options.name: goal_location
options.description: Which location name to consider to be the goal.

options.name: disable_chunk_culling
options.description:
Disable the culling that reduces the number of chunks that are in the "playable" space
DO NOT DO THIS UNLESS YOU HATE YOURSELF MORE THEN A NORMAL OSRS PLAYER

Useful will convert the chunks to useful, this means they won't actually be removed just not considered for logic

options.name: disable_task_culling
options.description:
Disable the culling that reduces the number of Tasks that get created
This might create much smaller spheres, if this becomes a problem I will have to find a way to tone it down more

options.name: max_drop_rate
options.description:
The Maximum drop rate that will be considered logical access
Be careful as to low a value might make your game unbeatable or at least VERY convoluted

options.name: full_drop_rate
options.description:
Override for Maximum Drop Rate that allows for choosing values that are VERY ill advised
Leave at 0 if you don't know what you're doing
Use with extreme caution

options.name: maximum_training_levels
options.description: The maximum levels that you will be expected to train each skill

options.name: starting_skill_levels
options.description: The starting levels that your character has prior to starting the multiworld

options.name: qp_per_level
options.description: The Number of quest points to increase the training range

options.name: levels_per_qp
options.description: The number of levels to be expected to be over trained for each set of quest points

options.name: base_training_levels
options.description: The Number of levels over a given training method you would be expected to train over by default

options.name: tutorial_island_items
options.description: Whether to keep or discard the starting inventory from tutorial island

options.name: pre_completed_tasks
options.description:
A list of location names that are completed before the game starts.
Useful for cases where you need a quest to get to your starting area

options.name: banned_chunks
options.description:
A list of chunks that won't be created as part of generation

Uses internal names (e.g. "chunk_12338" not "South Draynor")
But does count all sub-chunks (so you can just enter "chunk_8496" rather then "chunk_8496-1")

options.name: brutal_grinds
options.description:
Whether to allow skill tasks without having reasonable access to the usual skill training path.
For example, if enabled, you could be forced to train smithing without an anvil purely by smelting bars,
or training fishing to high levels entirely on shrimp.

options.name: progressive_tasks
options.description:
Whether skill tasks should always be generated in order of easiest to hardest.
If enabled, you would not be assigned "Mine Gold" without also being assigned
"Mine Silver", "Mine Coal", and "Mine Iron". Enabling this will result in a generally shorter seed, but with
a lower variety of tasks.

options.name: enable_duds
options.description: Whether to include filler "Dud" items that serve no purpose but allow for more tasks in the pool.

options.name: dud_count
options.description: How many "Dud" items to include in the pool. This setting is ignored if "Enable Duds" is not included

options.name: enable_carepacks
options.description:
Whether or not to include useful "Care Pack" items that allow you to trade over specific items.
Note: Requires your account NOT to be an Ironman. Also, requires access to another account to trade over the items,
or gold to purchase off of the grand exchange.

options.name: max_combat_level
options.description:
The highest combat level of monster to possibly be assigned as a task.
If set to 0, no combat tasks will be generated.

options.name: max_combat_tasks
options.description:
The maximum number of Combat Tasks to possibly be assigned.
If set to 0, no combat tasks will be generated.
This only determines the maximum possible, fewer than the maximum could be assigned.

options.name: combat_task_weight
options.description:
How much to favor generating combat tasks over other types of task.
Weights of all Task Types will be compared against each other, a task with 50 weight
is twice as likely to appear as one with 25.

options.name: max_prayer_tasks
options.description:

If set to 0, no Prayer tasks will be generated.
This only determines the maximum possible, fewer than the maximum could be assigned.

options.name: prayer_task_weight

options.name: minimum_general_tasks
options.description:
How many guaranteed general progression tasks to be assigned (total level, total XP, etc.).
General progression tasks will be used to fill out any holes caused by having fewer possible tasks than needed, so
there is no maximum.

options.name: general_task_weight
options.description:

Weights of all Task Types will be compared against each other, a task with 50 weight
is twice as likely to appear as one with 25.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
